intro: Platythelys roseoalba, also known as the "White Rose Orchid", is a rare, epiphytic orchid species native to tropical rainforests in Southeast Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, which may contribute to its use in traditional medicine for treating various health issues. The bioactive constituents of Platythelys roseoalba include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In traditional medicine, Platythelys roseoalba is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, and it is also used in the production of herbal teas and capsules.
